author: Irenaeus of Lyons
note: The rule of faith — the one apostolic faith held by the whole Church, stated in a trinitarian shape (one God the Father and Maker; one Christ Jesus, Son of God, incarnate for our salvation; the Holy Spirit who spoke through the prophets). Bounded excerpt of the long single sentence, ending at 'the dispensations of God' (the sentence continues with the advents, virgin birth, passion, resurrection, ascension). Cleanly orthodox; no Gnostic catalogue. Captured via web fetch (New Advent, Roberts–Donaldson ANF) and cross-checked against the known ANF wording; verify verbatim (incl. bracket in '[She believes]') in the citations pass.
text: The Church, though dispersed throughout the whole world, even to the ends of the earth, has received from the apostles and their disciples this faith: [She believes] in one God, the Father Almighty, Maker of heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all things that are in them; and in one Christ Jesus, the Son of God, who became incarnate for our salvation; and in the Holy Spirit, who proclaimed through the prophets the dispensations of God

author: Irenaeus of Lyons
note: One God the Creator — Irenaeus opens the refutation of the Gnostic systems by insisting the first and most important truth is God the Creator, maker of heaven and earth, against the Valentinian demiurge/pleroma scheme. Bounded excerpt (opening of II.1.1). Captured via web fetch (New Advent, Roberts–Donaldson ANF) and cross-checked against the known ANF wording; verify verbatim in the citations pass.
text: It is proper, then, that I should begin with the first and most important head, that is, God the Creator, who made the heaven and the earth, and all things that are therein

author: Irenaeus of Lyons
note: Apostolic succession/tradition stated in its NEUTRAL form: the tradition of the apostles is public and traceable through the succession of bishops instituted by the apostles. Deliberately drawn from III.3.1, NOT the adjacent III.3.2 'every Church should agree with this Church [Rome], on account of its pre-eminent authority' line — that clause is a contested papal-primacy proof-text (disputed rendering of 'potiorem principalitatem') and would cut against the site's denominational-neutrality guardrail. Bounded excerpt ending at a clause boundary ('to our own times'). Captured via web fetch (New Advent, Roberts–Donaldson ANF) and cross-checked against the known ANF wording; verify verbatim (incl. bracket in '[to demonstrate]') in the citations pass.
text: It is within the power of all, therefore, in every Church, who may wish to see the truth, to contemplate clearly the tradition of the apostles manifested throughout the whole world; and we are in a position to reckon up those who were by the apostles instituted bishops in the Churches, and [to demonstrate] the succession of these men to our own times

author: Irenaeus of Lyons
note: The fourfold Gospel — the Gospels can be neither more nor fewer than four (the classic patristic testimony to the four-Gospel canon, c. AD 180). Note the allusion 'the pillar and ground of the Church' quotes 1 Tim 3:15; confirm whether ANF sets it in quotation marks in the citations pass. Captured via web fetch (New Advent, Roberts–Donaldson ANF) and cross-checked against the known ANF wording ('four zones of the world', rendering Gk. klimata); verify verbatim in the citations pass.
text: It is not possible that the Gospels can be either more or fewer in number than they are. For, since there are four zones of the world in which we live, and four principal winds, while the Church is scattered throughout all the world, and the pillar and ground of the Church is the Gospel and the spirit of life; it is fitting that she should have four pillars, breathing out immortality on every side, and vivifying men afresh.

author: Irenaeus of Lyons
note: Recapitulation — 'He who was the Son of God became the Son of man' so that man might receive the adoption and 'become the son of God.' The passage itself fences the meaning as ADOPTION ('receiving the adoption'), i.e. sonship by grace/participation, not becoming God by nature; frame it that way to avoid a compressed reader hearing ontological apotheosis. Captured via web fetch (New Advent, Roberts–Donaldson ANF) and cross-checked against the known ANF wording; verify verbatim in the citations pass.
text: For it was for this end that the Word of God was made man, and He who was the Son of God became the Son of man, that man, having been taken into the Word, and receiving the adoption, might become the son of God.

author: Irenaeus of Lyons
note: The Eucharist and the resurrection of the flesh — as the bread of earth, receiving God's invocation, is 'no longer common bread' but 'consisting of two realities, earthly and heavenly,' so our bodies, receiving the Eucharist, are 'no longer corruptible, having the hope of the resurrection.' Load-bearing point for the goodness of the material creation and the bodily resurrection, against Gnostic contempt for the flesh. Use for the resurrection-of-the-body case; do NOT deploy to adjudicate intra-Christian eucharistic-mode debates (neutrality guardrail). Captured via web fetch (New Advent, Roberts–Donaldson ANF) and cross-checked against the known ANF wording; verify verbatim in the citations pass.
text: For as the bread, which is produced from the earth, when it receives the invocation of God, is no longer common bread, but the Eucharist, consisting of two realities, earthly and heavenly; so also our bodies, when they receive the Eucharist, are no longer corruptible, having the hope of the resurrection to eternity.

author: Irenaeus of Lyons
note: The classic 'admirable exchange' formula of recapitulation — the Word of God 'did, through His transcendent love, become what we are, that He might bring us to be even what He is Himself.' Presupposes Christ's full deity (only God can raise us to His own life) and full humanity (He truly became what we are). Frame the second clause as participation/likeness by grace, not becoming God by nature. Captured via web fetch (New Advent, Roberts–Donaldson ANF) and cross-checked against the known ANF wording; verify verbatim in the citations pass.
text: the Word of God, our Lord Jesus Christ, who did, through His transcendent love, become what we are, that He might bring us to be even what He is Himself.
